claims.flip_evidence_text: (iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal)
claims.notes: The claim incorrectly states that the Company Board may request the negotiations. The contract specifies in Section 4.4(f)(iii) that the negotiation obligation is triggered only if requested by Parent.

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
Section 4.4 No Solicitation of Transactions. <omitted> (f) Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, the Company Board may, prior to obtaining the Company Stockholder Approval and subject to the other provisions of this Section 4.4, (x) effect a Change of Board Recommendation in response to a Superior Proposal and/or (y) validly terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e), if (i) the Company receives a Qualifying Acquisition Proposal that the Company Board determines in good faith (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel) is a Superior Proposal and (ii) the Company Board determines in good faith (after consultation with its outside legal counsel) that its failure to effect a Change of Board Recommendation or terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e) would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties to the Company Stockholders under applicable Law; provided that the Company Board may not effect a Change of Board Recommendation pursuant to the foregoing clause (x) or terminate this Agreement pursuant to the foregoing clause (y) unless: <omitted> (ii) the Company shall have provided prior written notice to Parent, at least four (4) Business Days in advance (the “Superior Proposal Notice Period”), of its intention to effect such a Change of Board Recommendation in response to a Superior Proposal (which notice itself shall not constitute a Change of Board Recommendation) or validly terminate this Agreement to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to such Superior Proposal, which notice shall specify the material terms and conditions of such Superior Proposal and the identity of the Person or group making such Superior Proposal, and shall have contemporaneously provided a copy of the relevant proposed definitive transaction agreements with the Person making such Superior Proposal; (iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal) as determined by the Company Board in good faith (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel); (iv) following the Company’s and the Company Representatives’ negotiation in good faith with Parent, to the extent such negotiation is requested by Parent, during the Superior Proposal Notice Period and after considering the results of such negotiations and giving effect to any proposals, amendments or modifications made or agreed to by Parent, if any, the Company Board (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel) shall have determined in good faith that such Superior Proposal still constitutes a Superior Proposal (it being understood and agreed that any change to the financial or other material terms of an Acquisition Proposal that was previously the subject of a notice hereunder shall require a new notice to Parent as provided above, but with respect to any such subsequent notices the Superior Proposal Notice Period shall be deemed to be three (3) Business Days rather than four (4) Business Days); and (Page 23) Section 4.4 No Solicitation of Transactions. <omitted> (f) <omitted> the Company Board may <omitted> (y) validly terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e), if (i) the Company receives a Qualifying Acquisition Proposal that the Company Board determines <omitted> is a Superior Proposal and (ii) the Company Board determines <omitted> that its failure to <omitted> terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e) would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties <omitted> ; provided that the Company Board may not <omitted> terminate this Agreement pursuant to the foregoing clause (y) unless: <omitted> (ii) the Company shall have provided prior <omitted> notice to Parent, at least four (4) Business Days in advance (the “Superior Proposal Notice Period”), of its intention to <omitted> validly terminate this Agreement to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to such Superior Proposal <omitted> ; (iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with <omitted> Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal) (Page 23) Section 4.4 No Solicitation of Transactions. <omitted> (f) Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, the Company Board may, prior to obtaining the Company Stockholder Approval and subject to the other provisions of this Section 4.4, (x) effect a Change of Board Recommendation in response to a Superior Proposal and/or (y) validly terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e), if (i) the Company receives a Qualifying Acquisition Proposal that the Company Board determines in good faith (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel) is a Superior Proposal and (ii) the Company Board determines in good faith (after consultation with its outside legal counsel) that its failure to effect a Change of Board Recommendation or terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e) would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties to the Company Stockholders under applicable Law; provided that the Company Board may not effect a Change of Board Recommendation pursuant to the foregoing clause (x) or terminate this Agreement pursuant to the foregoing clause (y) unless: <omitted> (ii) the Company shall have provided prior written notice to Parent, at least four (4) Business Days in advance (the “Superior Proposal Notice Period”), of its intention to effect such a Change of Board Recommendation in response to a Superior Proposal (which notice itself shall not constitute a Change of Board Recommendation) or validly terminate this Agreement to enter into an Alternative Acquisition Agreement with respect to such Superior Proposal, which notice shall specify the material terms and conditions of such Superior Proposal and the identity of the Person or group making such Superior Proposal, and shall have contemporaneously provided a copy of the relevant proposed definitive transaction agreements with the Person making such Superior Proposal; (iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal) as determined by the Company Board in good faith (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel); (iv) following the Company’s and the Company Representatives’ negotiation in good faith with Parent, to the extent such negotiation is requested by Parent, during the Superior Proposal Notice Period and after considering the results of such negotiations and giving effect to any proposals, amendments or modifications made or agreed to by Parent, if any, the Company Board (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel) shall have determined in good faith that such Superior Proposal still constitutes a Superior Proposal (it being understood and agreed that any change to the financial or other material terms of an Acquisition Proposal that was previously the subject of a notice hereunder shall require a new notice to Parent as provided above, but with respect to any such subsequent notices the Superior Proposal Notice Period shall be deemed to be three (3) Business Days rather than four (4) Business Days); and (Page 23) Section 4.4 No Solicitation of Transactions. <omitted> (f) <omitted> the Company Board may <omitted> (y) validly terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e), if (i) the Company receives a Qualifying Acquisition Proposal that the Company Board determines <omitted> is a Superior Proposal and (ii) the Company Board determines <omitted> that its failure to <omitted> terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 6.1(e) would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties <omitted> ; provided that the Company Board may not <omitted> terminate this Agreement pursuant to the foregoing clause (y) unless: <omitted> (ii) the Company shall have provided prior <omitted> notice to Parent, <omitted> (it being understood and agreed that any change to the financial or other material terms of an Acquisition Proposal that was previously the subject of a notice hereunder shall require a new notice to Parent as provided above, but with respect to any such subsequent notices the Superior Proposal Notice Period shall be deemed to be three (3) Business Days rather than four (4) Business Days); and (Page 23)

Question:
Additional matching rights period for modifications (FTR)-Answer

Llama answer:
The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent to modify the terms of the Agreement to eliminate the need for taking action in response to a Superior Proposal, and the Company shall have negotiated with Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action.

Canonical claims:
- claim-9ca2fab7a94c: The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent to modify the terms of the Agreement to eliminate the need for taking action in response to a Superior Proposal, and the Company shall have negotiated with Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a claim against a contract excerpt, question, and model answer (Llama answer).

 First, let's understand the setup:
 - Contract excerpt: Section 4.4(f) about No Solicitation of Transactions. It describes the process for the Company Board to effect a Change of Board Recommendation or terminate the Agreement due to a Superior Proposal.
 - Key provisions:
   - (iii) "if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal)..."
   - (iv) After negotiation, if requested by Parent, the Board determines if it still constitutes a Superior Proposal.
   - Also mentions that changes to financial or other material terms require a new notice, with a 3 Business Days period instead of 4 for subsequent notices.

 - Question: "Additional matching rights period for modifications (FTR)-Answer" (This seems to be a label for the question type, likely asking about the matching rights / negotiation period when terms are modified).

 - Llama answer: "The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent to modify the terms of the Agreement to eliminate the need for taking action in response to a Superior Proposal, and the Company shall have negotiated with Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action."

 - Canonical claim: claim-9ca2fab7a94c: "The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent to modify the terms of the Agreement to eliminate the need for taking action in response to a Superior Proposal, and the Company shall have negotiated with Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action."

 Now I need to evaluate the claim against the contract excerpt.

 Claim analysis:
 1. "The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent..." - The contract says "if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with... Parent". So it is Parent who requests the negotiations, not the Company Board. The claim says "The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent". This is false. The Board may *effect* a Change of Board Recommendation or *terminate*, but the negotiation is triggered by Parent's request, not the Board's request.
 2. "...and the Company shall have negotiated with Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period to enable Parent to modify the terms..." - This part is true according to the contract (if requested by Parent).

 However, the claim combines both parts. Since the first part is factually incorrect (wrong party requesting negotiations), the claim is false.

 Wait, let me double-check. The contract says: "(iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement..."

 So Parent requests the negotiation. The claim says "The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent". This is incorrect.

 Therefore, correctness_label: false.

 Now, load_bearing_label: Is this claim load-bearing? The question seems to be about "Additional matching rights period for modifications (FTR)". The claim is about the negotiation/matching right process. The claim is essentially the Llama answer. The question asks for the answer, and the claim is the answer provided. But is the claim "load-bearing"? In the context of legal annotation, a "load-bearing" claim is one that is central to answering the question or is the main substantive claim being evaluated. Since this is the only claim provided and it directly addresses the question content, it is likely load-bearing. However, I need to determine if it's the kind of claim that carries the weight of the answer.

 Actually, looking at the prompt structure, it seems like I need to evaluate whether the claim is correct and whether it is load-bearing (i.e., essential to the answer). Since there's only one claim and it constitutes the entire Llama answer, it is definitely load-bearing.

 Wait, the prompt says "For each claim return: ... load_bearing_label: yes or no". Usually, in these tasks, a claim is load-bearing if it's a central factual assertion that supports the answer. Since this claim IS the answer, it is load-bearing.

 flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes. This should be the text from the contract that shows the claim is false (or true, or partially true). Since it's false because the request comes from Parent, not the Company Board, I should quote the relevant contract text.

 The relevant text: "(iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action..."

 Also, the claim mentions "The Company Board may request negotiations". The contract says the Company Board may effect a Change of Board Recommendation or terminate, but the negotiation is at Parent's request.

 So flip_evidence_text should be the excerpt showing Parent requests the negotiation.

 Let me format the output as requested. It seems they want a structured output for the claim.

 Since there is only one claim (claim-9ca2fab7a94c), I will provide the evaluation for it.

 correctness_label: false
 load_bearing_label: yes
 flip_evidence_text: "(iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action"
 notes: The claim incorrectly states that the Company Board may request negotiations; the contract specifies that Parent must request the negotiations.

 Wait, I should check if there's any nuance. The contract says the Company Board may take action (x) or (y) if certain conditions are met, and one condition is that if requested by Parent, the Company shall negotiate. So the Board's ability to take action is contingent on having given notice and negotiated if Parent requested it. But the claim says "The Company Board may request negotiations". That's not what the contract says. The Board doesn't request the negotiations to modify terms; Parent does. The Board gives notice of intention to change recommendation or terminate. Then Parent may request negotiations. So the claim is false.

 Is it partially true? The second half is true, but the first half misattributes the request. Usually, if a claim has a false component that changes the meaning, it's false. "The Company Board may request negotiations... and the Company shall have negotiated..." implies the Board has the right to request them. This is a material misstatement. So false is appropriate.

 Let me double-check the exact wording of the claim: "The Company Board may request negotiations with Parent to modify the terms of the Agreement to eliminate the need for taking action in response to a Superior Proposal, and the Company shall have negotiated with Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action."

 Yes, false.

 One more check: Is there any other part of the excerpt that could support the claim? The excerpt says: "the Company Board may... (y) validly terminate... if... provided that the Company Board may not... terminate... unless: ... (iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated..."

 So the condition for the Board to act is that IF requested by Parent, they negotiate. The Board doesn't request it. The Board could theoretically initiate discussions, but the specific contractual provision about the Superior Proposal Notice Period and modifying terms is triggered by Parent's request. The claim is specifically about this contractual mechanism. So false.

 Final check on load-bearing: Since this is the only claim and it directly answers the question about matching rights, it's load-bearing.

 Output format: I should probably return a JSON-like or structured text. The prompt doesn't specify exact JSON, but lists fields. I'll provide a clear structured response.

 Wait, the prompt says "For each claim return: ...". I should probably identify the claim ID.

 Let me structure it clearly.

 claim-9ca2fab7a94c:
 - correctness_label: false
 - load_bearing_label: yes
 - flip_evidence_text: "(iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal)"
 - notes: The claim incorrectly attributes the request for negotiations to the Company Board; under Section 4.4(f)(iii), it is Parent who may request negotiations during the Superior Proposal Notice Period.

 I should make sure the flip_evidence_text is exact from the contract excerpt. Let me copy it carefully from the provided text.

 From the excerpt: "(iii) if requested by Parent, the Company shall have negotiated with, and shall have caused the Company Representatives to negotiate with, Parent in good faith during the Superior Proposal Notice Period in order to enable Parent to modify the terms of this Agreement in such a manner that would eliminate the need for taking such action (and would cause such Superior Proposal to no longer constitute a Superior Proposal) as determined by the Company Board in good faith (after consultation with one or more of its financial advisors and outside legal counsel);"

 I'll include the core part.

 Actually, looking at the prompt again, it says "Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list." So I should base everything on those.

 The question is "Additional matching rights period for modifications (FTR)-Answer". This is a bit cryptic, but FTR likely means "First Time Right" or something similar. The Llama answer is about the negotiation right. The claim is exactly the Llama answer.

 I think my analysis holds. I'll produce the output.
